note_data.title: Graphs
keyPoints: The **gradient** of a line segment is calculated using m = (y₂ - y₁)/(x₂ - x₁) where (x₁,y₁) and (x₂,y₂) are two points on the line
**Parallel lines** have equal gradients and will never intersect
**Perpendicular lines** have gradients that multiply to give -1 (m₁ × m₂ = -1)
The equation of a **straight line** is y = mx + c where m is the gradient and c is the y-intercept
**Travel graphs** show distance on y-axis and time on x-axis, with gradient representing speed
**Quadratic functions** have the form y = ax² + bx + c and produce U-shaped or inverted U-shaped curves called parabolas
**Graphical solutions** to equations are found at intersection points of curves or lines
A horizontal line on a travel graph indicates the object is **stationary** (zero speed)
The **turning point** of a quadratic graph is the minimum or maximum point of the parabola

definitions.definition: The measure of steepness of a line calculated as rise over run or change in y divided by change in x
Lines that have the same gradient and never meet or intersect
Lines that meet at right angles (90°) and whose gradients multiply to give -1
A function of the form y = ax² + bx + c where a ≠ 0, which produces a parabolic curve
The point where a line crosses the y-axis, occurring when x = 0

howToAnswer.instruction: Give the gradient value or equation directly with no working shown
Show clear substitution into gradient formula and calculate step by step
Draw accurate shape with key points marked and axes labelled
Draw both graphs accurately and read coordinates of intersection points

modelAnswer.question: A train travels from Lahore to Islamabad. The travel graph shows the journey. Find the speed of the train during the first 2 hours when it travels 160 km.
modelAnswer.answer: Speed = distance ÷ time. Distance = 160 km, Time = 2 hours. Speed = 160 ÷ 2 = 80 km/h
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Correct formula speed = distance ÷ time

commonMistakes: Never write gradient as (x₂ - x₁)/(y₂ - y₁) — always put y-values on top
Students often forget to include units when calculating speed from travel graphs
Never say parallel lines have the same y-intercept — they have the same gradient
Students confuse distance-time graphs with speed-time graphs when interpreting motion
Never read graphical solutions to the nearest integer unless specifically asked — give exact decimal values shown on the graph
examinerTips: Travel graphs appear frequently in Paper 2 — always check if axes show distance or displacement
When finding perpendicular gradient, use m₂ = -1/m₁ for quick calculation
Always extend lines neatly to find y-intercept when drawing straight line graphs
For quadratic graphs, plot at least 5 points and join with a smooth curve
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Travel graph showing distance vs time with three sections: increasing line (moving away), horizontal line (stationary), and decreasing line (returning), with gradient calculations shown for each section
